title: Chris Mason: Burnham is fifth PM in four years - now he'll try to buck the trend
description: Determined to hit the ground running, the former mayor hopes to maintain the leadership style he adopted in Manchester.
summary: Chris Mason reports on the new Prime Minister, Burnham, who is the fifth leader in four years, and his plans to break the trend of frequent leadership changes. Burnham, a former mayor, aims to maintain his leadership style from Manchester.

title: Victory for football? Spain beat Argentina to win World Cup
description: It might seem like a trite cliche, but you won't find many neutrals disagreeing with that verdict on the World Cup final.
summary: Spain won the World Cup by beating Argentina in the final, with the victory being widely celebrated. The match was a highly anticipated event, and the outcome was met with little disagreement. The World Cup is an international football tournament.

title: Moment fans in Madrid celebrate World Cup win
description: Thousands of spectators watched Spain's victory in central Madrid on late Sunday night.
summary: Thousands of fans in Madrid celebrated Spain's World Cup win on Sunday night, with thousands of spectators gathering in the city to watch the victory. The atmosphere was electric as the team secured their championship title.

title: Broadcaster and journalist Edward Stourton dies aged 68
description: Stourton hosted Radio 4's Today programme for a decade and was a founding member of Channel 4 News.
summary: Edward Stourton, a well-known British broadcaster and journalist, has passed away at the age of 68. He had a long career in journalism, hosting Radio 4's Today programme and being a founding member of Channel 4 News.

title: Trump says US strikes hit Iran in 'honour' of American soldiers killed
description: Iran responded with attacks in Syria and Jordan, as two ships reportedly exploded in the Strait of Hormuz.
summary: US strikes in Iran were carried out in honor of American soldiers killed, leading to retaliatory attacks in Syria and Jordan, and explosions in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: My fitness tracker knew I was pregnant before I did
description: Ravika is one of hundreds of women who are noticing early pregnancy signs through the data on their fitness trackers.
summary: A growing number of women are discovering early signs of pregnancy through data on their fitness trackers, with some noticing changes before they even realize they are pregnant. This phenomenon is being reported by hundreds of women worldwide. Fitness trackers are providing valuable insights into women's health.

title: ‘Everyone's afraid to die’: Struggle to recruit soldiers in Ukraine turns violent
description: Aggression against recruitment officers has become increasingly threatening, police say.
summary: In Ukraine, the struggle to recruit soldiers has turned violent, with recruitment officers facing aggressive threats, according to police. The situation has become increasingly tense, with many people reportedly afraid to join the military.

title: Fire crews battle blaze on Arthur’s Seat in Edinburgh
description: A huge plume of smoke could be seen from as far away as Fife on Sunday evening.
summary: Fire crews battled a blaze on Arthur's Seat in Edinburgh, with smoke visible from as far away as Fife on Sunday evening. The exact cause of the fire is not specified in the article. The incident drew attention from nearby residents.

title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
description: Eight people die and dozens are injured as several cities are hit by missile and drone attacks, while Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.
summary: Russia has launched a major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities, resulting in eight deaths and dozens of injuries. The attack comes as Ukraine continues its assault on Russian infrastructure. The situation remains tense in the region.

title: Thames Water lenders preparing legal challenge in event of Burnham nationalisation
description: The BBC understands creditors would would pursue payment in full of the outstanding debts, in the multi-billions of pounds.
summary: Thames Water's lenders are preparing to take legal action if the UK government decides to nationalize the company, seeking full payment of their outstanding debts, which are estimated to be in the multi-billions of pounds.
